title: Today's Tech Brief: Here’s seven bloody minutes of Wolverine on the PS5
summary: A brief look at the latest tech news, including a lengthy gameplay trailer for Marvel's Wolverine on PS5 and UCLA's pre-litigation resolution with Oracle.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you today. First up, let's talk about gaming. If you're a fan of the Marvel universe and have been waiting for the Wolverine game on PS5, you're in luck. A new trailer has dropped, and it's a doozy – seven minutes long, to be exact. I mean, who doesn't love a good Wolverine game? The gameplay trailer gives us a bloody good look at what to expect from the game, and I'm excited to see how it will live up to the hype.

According to The Verge, the gameplay trailer for Marvel's Wolverine on PS5 has been released, giving fans a good look at what to expect from the game. The big takeaway here is that the game is set to launch in September. I'm curious to see how the gameplay will pan out – Wolverine is an iconic character, and I'm sure fans are eager to get their hands on it.

Next, I came across a story about UCLA and Oracle. Apparently, UCLA is seeking a pre-litigation resolution with Oracle over a delayed SaaS transformation project. This one caught my eye because it highlights the complexities of large-scale software projects and the potential risks involved. It's a reminder that even with the best planning, things can go awry. I guess that's what happens when you're dealing with massive institutions and complex technology.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
